topic: Someone finds their parent's draft letter of resignation from decades ago
raw_output:
[story 1]

The brittle, yellowed paper felt strangely cold beneath my fingertips. It was tucked inside the false bottom of an old steamer trunk my grandmother had insisted I clear out. Unfurling it revealed elegant, looping script—my father’s handwriting. A resignation letter. Dated 1978. My breath hitched, catching somewhere tight in my chest. The ink seemed impossibly sharp against the aged parchment. Every word swam before my eyes, a sudden, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet afternoon. I reread the final sentence, the signature a ghost of a man I barely knew. A tremor ran through my hand, causing the letter to tremble on the dusty floorboards. I needed to put it down, to shove it back into the shadows of the trunk, but my feet felt rooted to the spot, unable to move toward the exit.

[story 2]

I traced the faded ink with a fingertip, the texture rough and fragile. It was a document, official and damning, detailing a departure from a life I thought I understood. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. The room seemed to shrink around me; the shadows in the corners elongated into menacing shapes. I pulled my hand back as if burned, my knuckles white. The air in the attic suddenly felt thick, heavy, pressing down on my lungs. I looked from the letter to the dusty windowpane, trying to find an anchor in the mundane reality of the afternoon, but the silence was loud, echoing with unspoken history. I couldn't seem to draw a full breath.

[story 3]

When I finally unfolded the paper, the crispness of the old ink seemed to vibrate under my gaze. It was a formal statement, a final goodbye written by the man who was supposed to be a pillar of my family. A coldness seeped into my extremities, a sudden chill that had nothing to do with the temperature of the old house. My gaze darted from the signature to the date, then back again, searching for some hidden meaning, some explanation for this sudden, unwelcome revelation. A shallow gasp escaped my lips. I stood motionless, every muscle in my body tight, fighting an overwhelming urge to bolt from the room, to flee the weight of that forgotten correspondence.
